# Contributing to unifi-common-addons

First time contributing to unifi-common-addons? Read our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/unifi-utilities/unifi-common-addons/blob/main/.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#code-of-conduct)

### Discuss, ask questions about or disagree with changes in unifi-common-addons

- [unifi-utilities/discussions](https://github.com/orgs/unifi-utilities/discussions) is the place for community discussions around unifi-common-addons.
- Our issues and pull requests are for maintainers and contributors to discuss work to be done, not users or contributors asking questions about unifi-common-addons usage or disagreeing with changes already made.

### Report a bug

- Ideally, open a pull request to fix it, describing both your problem and your proposed solution.
- If not, open an issue on the relevant repository, but do not open both an issue and a pull request.

### Propose a feature

- Ideally, open a pull request to implement it, describing both the problem it solves for you and your proposed solution.
- If not, open an issue with a detailed description of your proposed feature, the motivation for it and alternatives considered.
- Please note we may close this issue or ask you to create a pull request if this is not something we see as sufficiently high priority.

### Artificial Intelligence/Large Language Model (AI/LLM) usage

We allow you to create issues and pull requests with AI/LLM with the following requirements:

- You must disclose in the initial issue or pull request that you used AI/LLM and what tool/model/etc. you used.
- You must review all AI/LLM generated code, prose, etc. content before you ask anyone to review it for you.
- You must be able to address all pull request review comments, manually if the AI/LLM cannot do so for you.
- Unless you are a maintainer, you may only have one AI-assisted/generated pull request open at a time.
- If you reach the point where you feel unwilling or unable to do the above, please close your issue or pull request.

Thanks!
